messages.content: isn't it wild how a coffee that was like 2 bucks when i was a teenager is basically 6 now

That gap between what things used to cost and what they cost now can feel almost disorienting — like the world quietly shifted while you weren't looking. It's one of those small, concrete ways time makes itself felt.

Does it land more as nostalgia, or something closer to frustration?
i was telling my coworker, feels like everything just quietly crept up and we all kinda accepted it

There's something almost eerie about that — the slow creep that never quite triggers an alarm because each step is small enough to absorb. And then one day you're paying six dollars for something without really questioning it.

What made you notice it today, do you think?
makes me a little nostalgic for the days when a 20 in your pocket felt like real money lol

That feeling of a twenty being substantial — almost like a different relationship with money entirely. It's not just the number, it's what it represented about possibility.

Is it mostly the money itself you're missing, or something more about that time in general?
anyway not complaining really, just one of those shower thoughts about how prices change over time
Those little observations have a way of carrying more weight than they seem to — a six-dollar coffee that's really about time passing. Good shower thought.
